summary: Recut enforcement-mode values {Shared,Surface}→{note,warn,block}: Shared→warn, Surface→block, note is the new out-of-band-only guard tier, default warn (0006)

per.section: The placements and their enforcement modes

edit.description: Recut `EnforcementMode` from `{Shared, Surface}` to `{Note, Warn, Block}` (the guard's three-value vocabulary, split by where the finding goes); move the `#[default]` to `Warn`. Rewrite the enum's doc comments: block denies the call, warn allows and surfaces in-band, note allows and records out-of-band only — drop the Shared-as-default prose. Keep the 0005 root-member cite; the value semantics cite is distribution.md 'The placements and their enforcement modes'.

Rewrite `guard()`'s mode→verdict map (~590): `Warn`→surface-in-band, `Block`→deny, `Note`→a new allow-but-defer verdict. Extend `GuardVerdict` (~574) with the note tier: allow the tool call (exit 0) with no in-band context injection — the finding is out-of-band only, riding the next `check`/report, never the live session. Update the `guard` doc (~586) to the block/warn/note channel split.

Recut `mode_from_lock` (~502): parse `"note"|"warn"|"block"` (was `"shared"|"surface"`) into the recut enum, default `warn` when absent/unrecognized. Fix the doc comments at ~339 and ~500 that name `shared`/`surface` as the lock's mode value and default.

The embedded built-in lock's mode fact (line ~176, `fact = "mode" value = "shared"`) recuts to `value = "warn"` — the first-party module's default mode under the new vocabulary. `tests/builtin_lock_frozen.rs` re-derives this from the module and byte-compares, so it must match the new SDK default.

Recut `EnforcementMode` (line 30) to `"note" | "warn" | "block"`; change the constructor default (line 69) `?? "shared"` to `?? "warn"`. Update the TSDoc (24-30, 43-54) to the block/warn/note channel split and the warn default. `declarations.ts:249` carries `harness.mode` through verbatim — no logic change, the new strings flow into the mode fact.

The `guard_reads_a_pretooluse_payload_and_acts_on_the_posture` test (~431-481) writes `[[declaration.assembly]] fact="mode" value="surface"` and asserts a projection write is blocked — recut the value to `"block"`. Cover the recut vocabulary: `block` denies (exit 2), `warn`/`note` allow the projection write.

The rich declaration-set test (~115, ~241-242, ~386-391) emits a `surface` enforcement mode and asserts the mode fact `value == "surface"` — recut the harness fixture to `block` and the assertion to `Some("block")`.

The generic `AssemblyFactRow` sample at line 236 (`value: Some("shared".to_string())`) uses a retired mode value — recut to a live value (`"warn"`). Clears the residue the MODE sweep parked to ride the next entry that opens this file.

tests.asserts: `temper guard` denies a `.claude/` projection write (exit 2) under a `block` lock, and allows it under `warn` and `note`.
A block-mode harness emits the assembly mode fact with value `block`.

acceptance: cargo fmt/clippy/test green; `EnforcementMode` is `{Note,Warn,Block}` default `Warn` across compose/install/main and the SDK type; the lock's mode fact reads note/warn/block; guard denies on block, surfaces in-band on warn, allows-and-defers on note; builtin_lock.toml carries value="warn" and builtin_lock_frozen passes.
notes: 0006 recuts the guard's mode vocabulary. Migration keeps shipped behavior: Shared→warn (allow+in-band surface), Surface→block (deny); `note` is the newly expressible tier (allow, record out-of-band only — next report, never the live session), unreachable until declared. Default warn; GuardVerdict gains the note tier. builtin_lock.toml's mode fact recuts shared→warn (builtin_lock_frozen re-derives+byte-compares). Also clears the tests/emit.rs:236 value="shared" residue the MODE sweep parked.

summary: Ship the three acquisition channels — SDK npm package, engine binary pinned via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone assets, the plugin/bundle — fail-loud on a missing platform binary

gate.reason: needs the per-platform engine-binary release workflow build-out + John's decide-at-release calls (optional Apple notarization for the standalone mac binary, USPTO name screen) before the mechanical wrappers validate end-to-end. No marketplace/signing creds exist — a marketplace is a git repo + marketplace.json, community submission a free web form gated by `claude plugin validate` (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs); npm creds done 07-05. SDK scope resolved (`@dtmd/temper` 0.0.3, single package, `(sdk-package-layout)`); channel 1's SDK foundation shipped. No `.github/workflows/release.yml` exists (only the `temper.yml` check job); root package.json is still the private flume manifest — nothing moved this window.

new.description: Build the per-target release binaries and publish them to the npm platform packages (channel 2: the engine binary pinned by the SDK via per-platform `optionalDependencies`, plus standalone GitHub release assets), and produce/publish the plugin bundle with its `marketplace.json` (channel 3). Channel auto-detected. Distinct from any CI *check* job, which is a documented two-line user-authored job (`temper check` + `emit --frozen` byte-compare), not a generated file (distribution.md).

edit.description: The root `package.json` today is the *private* flume control-plane manifest (`temper-flume-harness`, `private: true`). The npm distribution entry needs platform-specific `optionalDependencies` pointing at per-target prebuilt-binary packages plus a launcher that resolves and execs the platform binary, erroring loudly if none is installed (fail-loud, distribution.md). Reconcile with the flume manifest rather than clobbering it. NB distinct from sdk/package.json (`@dtmd/temper`, already published — the engine pin is declared there, once).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper via the SDK's pinned engine binary (per-platform optionalDependencies), standalone release assets, or the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); a missing platform binary is an install error, never a silent skip (distribution.md 'Fail-loud invariant').
notes: Release infra, mostly outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish; needs human creds. ch1 = SDK npm (`@dtmd/temper`, published 0.0.3); ch2 = engine binary via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone; ch3 = plugin/bundle via `temper bundle` + marketplace.json. Parked on human release creds + the engine-binary workflow. install.rs still pins SDK `^0.0.2` (0.0.3 published) — a release-owned bump, not this sweep.
